story.iWant: a parser that can read Nikon .np3 Picture Control binary files and extract all photo editing parameters
story.soThat: I can convert .np3 presets to other formats through the UniversalRecipe hub
story.tasks:
- Task 1: Create NP3 package structure (AC: #1, #5) - Create directory `internal/formats/np3/` - Create `parse.go` with package declaration and imports - Import `internal/models` for UniversalRecipe and validators - Add package-level documentation comment
- Task 2: Implement NP3 file validation (AC: #1) - Implement magic byte validation ("NP" at bytes 0-1) - Implement file size validation (minimum 1024 bytes) - Return descriptive errors for validation failures - Add helper function for initial file structure checks
- Task 3: Implement parameter extraction (AC: #2) - Research NP3 binary format byte offsets (document in comments) - Extract Sharpening from correct byte offset - Extract Contrast from correct byte offset - Extract Brightness from correct byte offset - Extract Saturation from correct byte offset - Extract Hue adjustment from correct byte offset - Document byte offsets in code comments for maintainability
- Task 4: Implement parameter validation (AC: #3) - Import validation functions from `internal/models/validation.go` - Call `ValidateNP3Sharpening()` for sharpening parameter - Call `ValidateNP3Contrast()` for contrast parameter - Call `ValidateNP3Brightness()` for brightness parameter - Call `ValidateNP3Saturation()` for saturation parameter - Call `ValidateNP3Hue()` for hue parameter - Return validation errors with parameter name context
- Task 5: Implement UniversalRecipe construction (AC: #4) - Import RecipeBuilder from `internal/models/builder.go` - Create new RecipeBuilder instance with `NewRecipeBuilder()` - Use builder methods to set extracted parameters - Call `Build()` method to get validated UniversalRecipe - Handle and return builder validation errors
- Task 6: Implement Parse() function (AC: #1-5) - Create `Parse(data []byte) (*models.UniversalRecipe, error)` signature - Validate file structure (Task 2 logic) - Extract all parameters (Task 3 logic) - Validate parameters (Task 4 logic) - Build UniversalRecipe (Task 5 logic) - Return UniversalRecipe or error
- Task 7: Write comprehensive tests (AC: #6) - Create `np3_test.go` in `internal/formats/np3/` - Implement `TestParse()` with table-driven pattern - Use `filepath.Glob()` to discover all `testdata/np3/*.np3` files - Run Parse() on each test file as subtest - Verify no parse errors on valid files - Verify extracted parameters are in valid ranges - Test invalid file: wrong magic bytes - Test invalid file: too small (<1024 bytes) - Test invalid file: out-of-range parameter - Run `go test -cover` and verify 95%+ coverage

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
1. NP3 File Structure Validation - Parse valid NP3 files without errors - Validate "NCP" magic bytes at file start (bytes 0-2) [Updated from "NP" based on reverse engineering] - Validate minimum file size of 300 bytes [Updated from 1024 bytes based on actual sample files] - Return clear error for invalid magic bytes or corrupted files - Error messages must be user-friendly (not just "parse failed")
2. Parameter Extraction - Extract Sharpening parameter (0-9 range, located at specific byte offset) - Extract Contrast parameter (-3 to +3 range) - Extract Brightness parameter (-1 to +1 range) - Extract Saturation parameter (-3 to +3 range) - Extract Hue adjustment parameter (-9° to +9° range) - All parameters mapped correctly to UniversalRecipe fields
3. Parameter Validation - Reuse NP3-specific validators from `internal/models/validation.go` - Validate parameter ranges during extraction - Return error if any parameter is out of valid range - Use inline validation (fail-fast pattern per architecture)
4. UniversalRecipe Construction - Use RecipeBuilder from `internal/models/builder.go` - Populate UniversalRecipe with extracted NP3 parameters - Use builder's fluent API for all parameter setting - Call `Build()` to get validated UniversalRecipe instance - Handle builder validation errors appropriately
5. Error Handling - Follow Pattern 5 (Error Handling) from architecture.md - Wrap errors with format-specific context - Include operation context ("parse NP3" vs "validate parameter") - Preserve underlying error for debugging with error wrapping - No panics - all errors returned via error return value
6. Test Coverage - Parse all 22 sample NP3 files from `testdata/np3/` directory - Table-driven tests following architecture Pattern 7 - Each test file runs as separate subtest - Achieve 95%+ code coverage for parse.go - Test edge cases: minimum file size, invalid magic, out-of-range parameters - Verify extracted parameters are within expected ranges

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Read and write Nikon Picture Control (.np3) binary files. Parameters: Sharpening (0-9), Contrast (-3 to +3), Brightness (-1 to +1), Saturation (-3 to +3), Hue (-9° to +9°). NP3 format is proprietary; reverse-engineered through binary analysis.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.section: Innovation - Reverse Engineering of Nikon .np3 Format
doc.snippet: First public documentation of Nikon's proprietary Picture Control format. Binary format decoder with byte-level precision, bidirectional conversion, 95%+ accuracy in format reconstruction. Round-trip testing validates accuracy.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: 22 NP3 files (Nikon Picture Control samples) available for testing in testdata directory. These represent official Nikon presets like Portrait, Landscape, Vivid.

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.section: Pattern 4: File Structure for Format Packages
doc.snippet: Every format package has identical structure: internal/formats/{format}/ with parse.go, generate.go, and tests. Signature: Parse([]byte) (*models.UniversalRecipe, error). Copy-paste pattern makes adding new formats easy.

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Always return wrapped ConversionError for conversion failures. Use fmt.Errorf() with %w verb for error wrapping. Include operation context in error messages. No panics - all errors via return value.

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Validate inline in parsers, fail fast with descriptive errors. Check magic bytes FIRST before any processing. Validate file size before attempting to read specific offsets. Clear, descriptive error messages for each failure type.

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Table-driven tests using real sample files from testdata/. Use filepath.Glob() to discover test files. Run each file as separate subtest with t.Run(). Target 95%+ code coverage.

doc.title: Story 1-1: Universal Recipe Data Model (Completed)
doc.snippet: UniversalRecipe struct available at internal/models/recipe.go with 50+ fields. Validation functions at internal/models/validation.go include NP3-specific validators. RecipeBuilder at internal/models/builder.go provides fluent API with method chaining and automatic validation.

artifact.reason: Hub data structure for all conversions. NP3 parser must populate this struct with extracted parameters.
NP3-specific validators for parameter ranges. Parser must call these during extraction to validate inline per architecture Pattern 6.
Fluent API for constructing UniversalRecipe. Parser uses this to build validated recipe from extracted NP3 parameters. Methods: WithSharpness(), WithContrast(), WithSaturation(), Build().
Reference implementation for testing patterns. NP3 parser tests should follow same table-driven approach with subtests.

constraints.constraint: Follow Pattern 4: File structure must match internal/formats/{format}/ with parse.go, generate.go, tests
Follow Pattern 5: Always wrap errors with fmt.Errorf("%w") to preserve error chain
Follow Pattern 6: Validate inline in parser - fail fast on invalid magic bytes, file size, or parameter ranges
Follow Pattern 7: Table-driven tests with filepath.Glob() to discover testdata files
Standard library only - no external dependencies for core parsing logic
Function signature must be: Parse([]byte) (*models.UniversalRecipe, error)
Import and reuse validators from internal/models/validation.go
Use RecipeBuilder fluent API for constructing UniversalRecipe
No panics - all errors returned via error return value
Document byte offsets in code comments for maintainability

tests.standards: Use Go's standard testing package with table-driven tests. Follow Pattern 7 from architecture: use filepath.Glob() to discover all test files in testdata/np3/. Run each file as a separate subtest with t.Run(). Target 95%+ code coverage. Use `go test -cover` to verify coverage. Test patterns established in internal/models/recipe_test.go show the expected structure with subtests and comprehensive edge case coverage.

ideas.test: Test valid NP3 file parses without error - validate magic bytes "NP" at offset 0-1
Test invalid magic bytes returns clear error message
Test file too small (less than 1024 bytes) returns descriptive error
Test all 22 sample NP3 files parse successfully with valid parameters
Verify extracted Sharpening is in range 0-9
Verify extracted Contrast is in range -3 to +3
Verify extracted Brightness is in range -1.0 to +1.0
Verify extracted Saturation is in range -3 to +3
Verify extracted Hue is in range -9 to +9
Test out-of-range parameter triggers validation error
Test returned UniversalRecipe has correct source format set to "np3"
Test builder validation errors are propagated correctly
Test wrapped errors include "parse NP3" context
Run `go test -cover` and verify coverage is 95%+ for parse.go
